strategies.pattern: Échapper le HTML via l'API DOM (document.createElement + textContent) au lieu de regex/replace
strategies.example: escapeHtml(str) { if (!str) return ''; const div = document.createElement('div'); div.textContent = str; return div.innerHTML; }

strategies.pattern: Chart.js instance lifecycle management — destroy before recreate OR use update pattern (preferred)
strategies.example: Preferred: if (chart) { chart.data = newData; chart.update(); } else { chart = new Chart(ctx, config); }. Alternative: if (chart) { chart.destroy(); chart = null; } chart = new Chart(ctx, config);

strategies.pattern: Cache sessionStorage pour les réponses API avec clé préfixée (éviter collisions)
strategies.example: sessionStorage.setItem('ec_' + key, JSON.stringify(data)) avec try/catch pour quota dépassé

strategies.pattern: User preference integration with compound prioritization — honor stated exercise preferences while ensuring compounds lead each session
strategies.example: Profile likes squats, no burpees → squats placed as primary Day 1+2 (sumo variant), burpees excluded entirely, mountain climbers as metabolic alternative.

strategies.pattern: Exercise sequencing by dominance emphasis rotation within full-body format
strategies.example: Day 1: Lower-focus (Squats→Lunges→Bridges→Push-ups). Day 2: Upper-focus (Push-ups→Sumo Squats→Superman→Dips). Day 3: Balanced. Prevents monotony while maintaining full-body stimulus.

strategies.pattern: Include deload week in progression cycle (typically week 6-7, 40% volume reduction)
strategies.example: Week 7 deload: reduce volume by 40%, maintain tempos, focus on mobility/recovery. Supports CNS recovery, reduces overtraining risk.

strategies.pattern: Tempo differentiation by exercise class — compound vs isometric vs isolation
strategies.example: Compound lifts: 5-6s tempos (3s ecc, 1s pause, 2s con). Isometric holds: static. Dynamic/isolation: 3s tempos. Explosive concentric (X) for heavy strength >80% 1RM.

strategies.pattern: Rep range periodization within single session — higher reps for compounds, lower for accessories (or inverted for strength goals)
strategies.example: Beginner weight loss: 15 reps (squats) → 12 reps (lunges) → 10 reps (push-ups). Strength: 3-5 reps (primaries) → 8-12 reps (accessories).

strategies.pattern: Dedicated safety protocol box for profiles with injuries/limitations — visually distinct section explaining all exercise modifications
strategies.example: Red-bordered box listing: barbell OHP removed (replaced by landmine press), incline pressing avoided, face pulls every pull day, stop-if-sharp-pain rule.

strategies.pattern: Sub-component decomposition for React fitness programs — separate ExerciseCard, DayPanel, ProgressionTable components
strategies.example: function ExerciseCard({ ex }) with priority border color, name, sets x reps, tempo, rest, technique badge, and note.

strategies.pattern: Lift-specific wave loading back-off patterns — vary the wave structure per lift based on CNS recovery cost
strategies.example: Bench: ascending-descending 72/77/82/82/77%. Squat: plateau 70/75/80/80/80%. Deadlift: peak-backoff 75/80/85/85/80%.

strategies.pattern: Anti-movement core training dominance for strength — prioritize anti-rotation, anti-extension, anti-lateral flexion over spinal flexion
strategies.example: Pallof Press, Ab Wheel Rollout, Copenhagen Plank, Suitcase Carry. Ratio 5:1 anti-movement:flexion for spine stability under load.

strategies.pattern: Occupation/lifestyle-aware exercise rationale in technique fields — tie exercise choices to user's job, daily activities, and specific physical demands
strategies.example: Night bartender: 'Counteract hip flexor tightness from standing shifts', 'Spinal stability for long shift protection', 'Shoulder strength for bartending'. PE teacher: 'Setting example for students'.

strategies.pattern: Mind-muscle connection (MMC) cue embedded in every exercise note for beginner/technique-focus profiles
strategies.example: Each exercise note includes: 'feel quads+glutes consciously', 'squeeze pecs at top', 'feel hamstrings stretch'. Unified tempo with documented 'EMG increase' justification.

strategies.pattern: Core anti-movement plane rotation across days — each session targets a different stability plane
strategies.example: Day A: anti-extension (Plank). Day B: anti-rotation (Bird Dog). Day C: anti-lateral flexion (Side Plank). Full coverage across 3 sessions.

strategies.pattern: Post-strength LISS cardio for fat-oxidation window in weight loss programs — 20 min low-intensity after resistance training
strategies.example: After strength work: 20 min bike or elliptical at 65-70% HR max. Exploits post-resistance fat-oxidation window. Different machines each session for variety.

strategies.pattern: Session-specific warmup and cooldown matching primary muscles — not generic warmup for all days
strategies.example: Squat day: hip circles + leg swings. Pull day: band pull-aparts + thoracic rotations. Push day: shoulder circles + push-up hold. Cooldown stretches match session muscles.

strategies.pattern: Antagonist superset pairing for time efficiency on isolation accessories
strategies.example: Bicep curl + skull crusher superset saves ~5 min per session. Cable fly + face pull superset. No rest penalty since antagonist muscles recover during agonist work.

strategies.pattern: CrossFit benchmark WODs as built-in progress tracking — use named WODs (Fran, Cindy) for automatic comparability across training weeks
strategies.example: W1-2: establish baseline Fran time. W3-4: beat W1-2 scores. W5: deload. W6: retest benchmarks. Athletes already know these WODs and care about scores.

strategies.pattern: Menopause/age-specific exercise justification linking each exercise to its physiological benefit mechanism
strategies.example: Each exercise note explains: 'Axial loading through femur stimulates osteoblasts' (squat), 'Ground reaction force 3-5x bodyweight' (step-ups), 'Fall prevention via proprioception' (single-leg balance).

strategies.pattern: Event delegation with data-action attributes instead of inline onclick in dynamically generated HTML
strategies.example: container.addEventListener('click', e => { const btn = e.target.closest('[data-action]'); if (btn) handleAction(btn.dataset.action, btn.dataset.id); });

strategies.pattern: Schema validation on localStorage reads with per-entry field validation and type guards
strategies.example: let habits = []; try { const raw = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('hf_habits') || '[]'); if (Array.isArray(raw)) habits = raw.filter(h => typeof h.id === 'string' && typeof h.name === 'string'); } catch { habits = []; }

strategies.pattern: Centralized init() function orchestrating all app setup (loadData, applyTheme, render, registerSW, initEventListeners) called from DOMContentLoaded
strategies.example: document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => { loadData(); applyDarkMode(); renderToday(); injectManifest(); registerSW(); initEventListeners(); });

strategies.pattern: Single centralized state object instead of scattered global variables for app state management
strategies.example: const state = { habits: [], logs: {}, notes: {}, settings: { dark: false } }; function saveData() { localStorage.setItem('app_state', JSON.stringify(state)); }

strategies.pattern: Centralized initEventListeners() function grouping ALL event bindings in one auditable place
strategies.example: function initEventListeners() { /* all tab clicks, form submits, toggles, delegated handlers in one place */ }

strategies.pattern: Auto-save text inputs with debounce instead of manual save button — reduces friction
strategies.example: let saveTimer; textarea.addEventListener('input', () => { clearTimeout(saveTimer); saveTimer = setTimeout(() => saveNote(dateKey, textarea.value), 600); });

strategies.pattern: ARIA roles and keyboard navigation for PWA accessibility (role=grid, role=listitem, tabindex, Enter/Space handlers)
strategies.example: card.setAttribute('role', 'listitem'); card.setAttribute('tabindex', '0'); card.addEventListener('keydown', e => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') toggle(id); });

strategies.pattern: Framing AI limitations as feature not bug — honest constraints section increases credibility more than perfect claims
strategies.example: Section 'L'honnêteté sur les limites' listing: AI doesn't replace coach, 34 patterns don't cover everything, quality depends on input, no longitudinal tracking yet.

strategies.pattern: Open-source transparency as credibility marketing — sharing frameworks on GitHub proves seriousness better than claims
strategies.example: 'Je développe mes outils en open source. Les frameworks sont sur GitHub. Le système immunitaire est documenté.'

strategies.pattern: Concrete error rates validate claims better than generic 'improved quality' — use real production numbers
strategies.example: 'Sur les programmes analysés, des problèmes ont été détectés dans près d'un cas sur deux' vs 'la qualité a été améliorée'

strategies.pattern: Direct-answer div with immediate credential establishes authority — question/answer format with expertise signal in first 4 lines
strategies.example:
Question: L'IA peut-elle...

Réponse: Oui, mais... [credential: coach diplômé d'État]

strategies.pattern: Mobile-first CSS Grid layout with responsive breakpoints — repeat(N, 1fr) at desktop, 1fr at mobile via @media
strategies.example: .kpi-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 20px; } @media (max-width: 768px) { .kpi-row { grid-template-columns: 1fr; } }

strategies.pattern: Explicit Chart.js color theming for dark backgrounds — set Chart.defaults.color, gridColor, tickColor globally instead of per-chart
strategies.example: Chart.defaults.color = '#8892b0'; const gridColor = 'rgba(255,255,255,0.06)'; // reuse in all chart options.scales

strategies.pattern: Prioritized hero image loading with fetchpriority=high + deferred lazy loading for secondary images
strategies.example: Hero: fetchpriority='high' + preload. Secondary: loading='lazy' + decoding='async' + R2 CDN

strategies.pattern: RequestAnimationFrame throttling with ticking flag to batch scroll events for parallax
strategies.example: if (!ticking) { requestAnimationFrame(updateParallax); ticking = true; } — passive: true on scroll listener

strategies.pattern: IntersectionObserver + unobserve for one-shot scroll-triggered animations
strategies.example: observer.observe(el) → on isIntersecting: add 'visible' class + unobserve (fires once, memory friendly)

strategies.pattern: Passive event listeners on high-frequency scroll handlers for browser optimization
strategies.example: window.addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true }) — lets browser optimize scrolling

strategies.pattern: Scroll-indexed shape morphing via modulo cycling — border-radius changes triggered by Math.floor(scrollY / interval) % shapes.length
strategies.example: var idx = Math.floor(scrollY / 120) % shapes.length; btn.style.borderRadius = shapes[idx].br; — 10 organic blob shapes with unique box-shadows

strategies.pattern: CSS class toggle (active/waiting) instead of display:none for scroll-driven visibility — preserves CSS transitions on reentry
strategies.example: .stack-item.active .carousel-img { opacity:1; transform:translateX(0); } .stack-item.waiting .carousel-img { opacity:0; transform:translateX(-120%); }

strategies.pattern: Credential-present implies validation-required — if a secret/key exists in env, the endpoint MUST verify it before proceeding
strategies.example: const key = request.headers.get('Authorization')?.replace('Bearer ', ''); if (key !== env.API_KEY) return new Response('Unauthorized', {status: 401}); // THEN process request

strategies.pattern: Fail-closed pattern for secrets — if secret is missing from env, reject request rather than falling through to default/open access
strategies.example: const secret = env.WEBHOOK_SECRET; if (!secret) { console.error('WEBHOOK_SECRET not configured'); return new Response('Server misconfigured', {status: 500}); }

strategies.pattern: All DB text fields require escapeHtml() before HTML template interpolation — never trust stored data in server-side rendering
strategies.example:
const title = escapeHtml(row.title); const html = `

${title}

`; // Even DB data could contain injected HTML from previous unvalidated input

strategies.pattern: Query params and path segments treated as hostile input — validate type, length, and format before use
strategies.example: const id = parseInt(url.searchParams.get('id')); if (isNaN(id) || id < 1 || id > 100000) return new Response('Invalid ID', {status: 400}); // Then use in prepared statement

strategies.pattern: Cost-bearing endpoints require auth BEFORE external API call — gate pattern prevents abuse charges
strategies.example:
// WRONG: const result = await anthropic.messages.create(...); if (!auth) return 401;
// RIGHT: if (!await verifyAuth(req)) return 401; const result = await anthropic.messages.create(...);

strategies.pattern: Persistent rate limiting via KV/Durable Objects instead of in-memory counters in serverless environments
strategies.example: const key = `rate:${ip}:${minute}`; const count = parseInt(await env.RATE_KV.get(key) || '0'); if (count > 60) return new Response('Too Many Requests', {status: 429}); await env.RATE_KV.put(key, String(count + 1), {expirationTtl: 120});

strategies.pattern: Multi-trigger picker init: rAF + window load + setTimeout safety net + resize reset — ensures init runs after paint on all mobile browsers
strategies.example: requestAnimationFrame(initPicker); window.addEventListener('load', initPicker); setTimeout(initPicker, 200); window.addEventListener('resize', () => { pickerReady = false; setTimeout(initPicker, 50); });

strategies.pattern: Velocity-based momentum projection for swipe snapping — track instantaneous velocity during drag, project final position, gives native-feel inertia
strategies.example: const proj = lonOff + lonDrag.v * 120; const ci = Math.round((lonW()/2 - ITEM_W/2 - proj) / ITEM_W); lonSnapTo(ci, true);

strategies.pattern: Exact calendar month calculation handling variable month lengths and leap years — avoids 30-day approximation errors in billing/duration contexts
strategies.example: let remDays = day2 - day1; if (remDays < 0) { months -= 1; remDays += new Date(y2, m2, 0).getDate(); } return months + remDays / daysInMth;

strategies.pattern: Proximity-based visual classes (center/near1/near2) on picker items — CSS-driven scale/opacity cascade without JS animation loop
strategies.example: const d = Math.abs(i - ci); el.classList.toggle('center', d===0); el.classList.toggle('near1', d===1); el.classList.toggle('near2', d===2);

strategies.pattern: Runtime container-width-relative offset for picker tape — offForIdx(i) uses element.offsetWidth at call time, survives resize and responsive layouts
strategies.example: function offForIdx(i) { return lonW() / 2 - ITEM_W / 2 - i * ITEM_W; } // lonW() = picker.offsetWidth — called live, not cached

strategies.pattern: Gradient fade ::before/::after pseudo-elements on picker edges — depth illusion with zero extra DOM nodes
strategies.example: .picker::before { left:0; background: linear-gradient(to right, var(--bg) 15%, transparent); } .picker::after { right:0; background: linear-gradient(to left, var(--bg) 15%, transparent); }

strategies.pattern: Clamp drag offset with elastic overshoot allowance (±0.6 * ITEM_W) — prevents tape from escaping bounds while allowing slight rubber-band feel at edges
strategies.example: function clampOff(off) { return Math.max(offForIdx(n-1) - ITEM_W*.6, Math.min(offForIdx(0) + ITEM_W*.6, off)); }

strategies.pattern: Abandon complex custom picker for native <input type='date'> when layout complexity outweighs benefit — saves hours, native works perfectly on mobile
strategies.example: After persistent display issues with custom date picker (offsetWidth=0 loop), replaced with native <input type='date' color-scheme='dark'> — zero bugs, handles leap years, calendar UI included

strategies.pattern: Dual-write transitoire pour migration de format sans downtime — écrire simultanément dans l'ancien format (JSON) et le nouveau (SQLite) pendant N sessions, valider la parité, puis basculer la lecture
strategies.example: Pendant 10 sessions: write → json ET sqlite. Après 10 sessions: assert(json_count == sqlite_count); switch reads to sqlite; disable json writes. Rollback: re-enable json reads if sqlite checksum fails.

strategies.pattern: File-lock externe pour SQLite write sérialisation multi-process — utiliser un lockfile pour sérialiser les writes de plusieurs agents parallèles sans modifier l'architecture des agents
strategies.example: const lock = require('proper-lockfile'); const release = await lock.lock('immune.sqlite.lock', {retries: 5, minTimeout: 100}); try { db.prepare(...).run(...); } finally { await release(); }

strategies.pattern: SQLite FTS5 comme alternative zero-dependency aux vecteurs pour recherche sémantique légère — BM25 natif couvre 80% des cas sans embedding, zero dépendance externe, query < 5ms sur 10K chunks
strategies.example: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE chunks_fts USING fts5(text, antibody_id, domain, tokenize='porter ascii'); SELECT antibody_id, rank FROM chunks_fts WHERE chunks_fts MATCH ? ORDER BY rank LIMIT 10;

strategies.pattern: Adapter pattern pour découpler les consommateurs du format de stockage — exposer une interface stable (getAntibodies, addAntibody, getStrategies) que tous les skills consomment, indépendamment du backend (JSON ou SQLite)
Score de qualité normalisé par domaine avec baseline calibrée sur données historiques — calculer mean/std sur les 100 derniers outputs par domaine avant de définir les seuils PASS/FAIL
strategies.example: domain_baselines: { code: { mean: 74.2, std: 8.3, pass_threshold: 65 }, fitness: { mean: 71.5, std: 11.2, pass_threshold: 60 } }. z_score = (score - mean) / std. PASS si score > (mean - 1*std).

strategies.pattern: Sanitizer de secrets avant indexation de fichiers de configuration — scanner les fichiers markdown avant chunking pour remplacer les patterns sensibles par des placeholders non-indexés
strategies.example: const SECRET_PATTERNS = [/[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}/g, /sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{32,}/g, /Bearer\s+[\S]+/g]; function sanitize(text) { return SECRET_PATTERNS.reduce((t, p) => t.replace(p, '[REDACTED]'), text); }
